# koyracloud

> koyracloud is an open-source, self-hosted Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) for Docker
> Swarm. You connect a git repo with a small `.paas/app.yaml` manifest and koyracloud
> builds a per-app container image, pushes it to a built-in registry, and runs it
> behind HTTPS — with injected secrets, persistent storage, custom domains,
> push-to-deploy, rollback, background workers, scheduled cron jobs and a per-app
> Redis bus. It is the "connect a repo and it deploys" experience of Vercel or
> Render, but it runs entirely on your own hardware. License: AGPL-3.0.

## What it does

- Connect a git repo; a `.paas/app.yaml` manifest describes build, start command,
  port, persistent dirs, env and secrets. Or bring your own `Dockerfile`.
- Each deploy builds a per-app image, pushes it to an internal registry, and runs a
  Docker Swarm service from it behind Traefik with automatic TLS.
- Every app gets a free `<name>-<token>.<apps-domain>` URL; attach custom domains via
  Cloudflare for SaaS or Let's Encrypt.
- Secrets are Fernet-encrypted at rest and injected at deploy. Persistent dirs live on
  NFS-backed volumes and survive redeploys.
- Push-to-deploy via GitHub webhook (optionally gated on CI), live build logs, deploy
  history and one-click rollback.
- Background work, all from the same repo and image: always-on `workers`, scheduled
  `cron` jobs (5-field UTC, run to completion), and a shared, per-app-isolated `redis`
  bus that injects `REDIS_URL` (keys/channels namespaced as `<app>:`).
- Built-in cookieless analytics and an uptime monitor per app.

## Who it is for

Single-operator and homelab use — trusted code, internal apps, client projects and
side projects. Not a multi-tenant public cloud.

## Self-hosting

- Repository: https://github.com/hikmahtech/koyracloud
- Requirements: a Docker Swarm with Traefik (HTTPS entrypoint + ACME resolver) and an
  NFS export reachable by the nodes.
- Quick start: clone the repo, configure `deploy/koyracloud.env` and the Docker secrets
  documented in `deploy/README.md`, then run `./deploy/deploy.sh` against your swarm.

## Links

- Source code: https://github.com/hikmahtech/koyracloud
- Documentation: https://koyracloud.com/docs
- Architecture & design: https://github.com/hikmahtech/koyracloud/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Migrating from Vercel: https://github.com/hikmahtech/koyracloud/blob/main/docs/MIGRATING-FROM-VERCEL.md
